title: hieracium subramosum subsp. fasciculare
intro: Hieracium subramosum subsp. fasciculare is a subspecies of the hawkweed plant, characterized by its yellow dandelion-like flowers and a hairy stem. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Hieracium subramosum subsp. fasciculare include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying the leaves and flowers, then infusing them in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
